desc:
As the river travels eastwards it roughly brushes against the riverbank, which
serves to turn its course to the south. As it rubs against the bank the flow of
the water begins to slow. Ahead in the distance the river is just a calm, smooth
trickle.
name: A Turn in the River

desc:
This narrow spit of earth, no more than a couple miles in width, stretches out
between the courses of two water streams. On the one hand flows the mighty
Anduin, taking a wide bend from east to south. On the other, a swift stream
comes swirling down the mountains, and bathes the northern bank before rushing
into the Great River. Soaked in water, the ground looks fertile indeed, and
vegetation grows luxuriant.
name: A Lush Tongue of Land

desc:
The Anduin bends between the plains on both sides of it. The flow is slowing
down again, after having broken out of the narrow bed to the north, and once
again the stream is taking up all its width. The river is still deep, but you
can guess, more than actually see, a rock-strewn bottom from the many eddies.
There is no sign of a fordable section anywhere nearby.
name: Bend in the River

desc:
A narrowing strip of land is squeezed between the River Anduin and a fast
flowing stream to the south and west. A number of ancient-looking willows bend
over the river, with their silvery leaves touching the waters. On the opposite
bank, a lone high rock can be seen, towering menacingly above the flow.
name: Stand of Willows

desc:
Here the stream takes its final turn, avoiding a low willow-lined peninsula,
before joining the flow of the Anduin a few miles ahead. Northwards, the ground
rises steeply to a promontory, the slope covered by low bushes and a few trees.
Further south, the ground is perfectly flat and covered with tall grass that
flows in the wind.
name: A Stream through the Valley

desc:
The mighty Anduin continues its way towards the Great Sea. As the riverbed is
becoming deeper, the flow strengthens. To the south, a lone high rock can be
seen breaking the flow of waters. Overlooking the wide river, you can see a vast
green prairie stretching out towards the horizon, with only a few low, lonely
hillocks rising from the flat ground.
name: The River Anduin

desc:
The flow of the waters to the west has been stopped by a steep slope, rising
almost vertically for more than fifteen feet and forcing the river to bend
southwards yet again. The other bank is lower and should lend itself to an easy
landing. Grasslands seem to continue all the way to the horizon over there.
name: Bend in the River Anduin

desc:
The western plains slowly descend until lining the Anduin with a narrow strip of
white sand. On the other side of the river, barely visible from here, the flat
ground stretches out all the way to the horizon, covered in lush green
grasslands. Only a few lonely hillocks, no more than a few feet high, rise out
of the seemingly endless plains there.
name: Grasslands by the Anduin

desc:
The plateau narrows into a steep promontory overlooking the lush valley below,
fed by a fast-flowing stream. To the west, the rocky ground drops vertically
into a series of roaring rapids pounding its walls. Across the stream,
south-west, a tall and lonely tree is standing atop the hill, its trunk split by
lightning.
name: A Rise Overlooking the Valley

desc:
The stream gets squeezed into a narrow canyon between towering stone walls on
both sides, forcing its waters higher and faster. Several sharp cliffs are
breaking the flow, the sound of water rushing past the boulders can be heard
hundreds of yards away. Southwards, the stream can be seen bending left in a
short while, looking a great deal more peaceful than here.
name: Turbulent Rapids

desc:
The northern plateau comes to an abrupt stop here, with a vertical cliff-face
dropping some twenty feet to a roaring chute below. The air is misty from
droplets of water thrown all the way up here by the flow of water hitting
boulders and canyon walls with abominable force. North-east, a battered stony
ridge can be seen running through the land, splitting it into a higher barren
plateau and lower sloping grasslands.
name: A Rocky Ledge

desc:
The barren image of northern grasslands changes is it is slightly enlivened by a
stream flowing south of here. Several bushes dot the landscape, and even the
grass is a lot taller and greener than those that can be seen farther away. To
the east, the stream has cut a gorge into a ridge running through the
grasslands, a few miles away from here.
name: Plains by the Stream

desc:
The stream continues to travel calmly along the plains of the surrounding
landscape. The water appears to get slightly deeper and an increase of the fish
inhabitants is more noticeable near the centre of the stream. Plains can be seen
to the east and west of you which seem very fertile given off by the presence of
the lively green grasslands.
name: Stream through the Plains

desc:
The stream seems to get slightly larger as it travels southward heading into
what one would predict to be a greater mass of water. The calmness of the stream
allows one to easily navigate back and forth through the waters and onto the
stream's shores. In the distance to the north lies many great plains in the
endless landscape. Small fish can be seen trying to swim along with the small
current of the stream water.
name: Stream through the Plains

desc:
The terrain here can be seen quickly ascending as it begins to stretch towards
the stream nearby. A few yards away, it levels off into a small terrace that
forms a viewpoint from which anyone could easily make simple observations.
Heading towards the small terrace, you can overlook the stream and glance at the
vast grasslands that encompass the surrounding land. A small crumbling building
can be seen in the distance to the south.
name: Steep Bank Overlooking the Stream

desc:
As the stream continues to flow eastward the pace of the water ever so slightly
speeds up as it bends to the south. As you go further along the small stream the
water level seems to get deeper, contouring to the earth's natural sloping
nature. An old crumbling building can be seen in the far distance to the south,
but gives off the impression of vacancy set by its rotting wooden structure and
its mossy covered walls.
name: Stream Bend in the Plains

desc:
A lonely majestic pine tree dominates the landscape, looking over the sloping
grassland spreading in all directions. West of here, the ground descends steeply
towards the valley, at the bottom of which a shallow but moderately paced stream
is flowing. The sandy ground is covered by many pine-needles and several cones
shed from tall but growing trees.
name: A Rising Ground

desc:
As the ground starts rising steeply eastwards the stream bends to the right,
finding its way to the bottom of the fertile valley. Few pines dot the landscape
and the grass is tall here, unlike up on the plains where it is beaten and bent
by a constant wind. Although the stream is rather narrow, the water is
swift-flowing and fording it can prove more difficult than it might initially
look.
name: A Stream through a Lush Valley

desc:
The ground is rising eastwards, to form a small hillock just about a mile away
from here. The vegetation seems to grow thicker in that direction, but here it
is just an odd bush among the wind-beaten grass. A wide river can be seen
flowing further to the east, with the opposite bank barely more than a line at
the horizon. To the west, a steep incline climbs to a rocky plateau. In all
other directions, sloping plains with a sea of tall grass flowing in the wind
covering them continue as far as an eye can see.
name: Barren Fields

desc:
Thick grass grows up to the waist-level of an average man here, flowing in eerie
waves in the relentless winds. Several unmistakable signs indicate that this
place has been a ground for a fierce battle sometime in the past. Occasional
cracked skulls, both orkish and those of men, bleached white by sun and rains
are lying around, half-hidden among the grass and the carcasses of horses and
wargs. Broken spears and swords alongside battered pieces of armour dot the
ground, slowly being overtaken by fast-growing grass which is well nourished by
the blood that has been shed here.
name: Waist-high Grass Fields

desc:
Several sharp rocks and boulders rise from waist-tall grass here, with low and
wind-bent bushes growing in their shadow, seeking a shelter from the wind.
Southwards, the grass becomes lower, and in a couple of miles, the fields
descend into a wide river, cutting through seemingly endless grass- lands that
continue in every direction. Few rain-polished rib cages of horses, almost
hidden in the tall grass, betoken a distant battle that has been fought here. A
prominent mound of rocks, covered with moss and small bushes, suggests that
someone important has fallen here.
name: Battered Fields

desc:
The main branch of the river continues on its way south as it runs past a huge
rock standing near the eastern bank. The flow is now faster, and the swirling
waters give hint of the treacherous boulders that lie on the riverbed. A swift
tributary stream comes from the west, leaving a small peninsula behind. On the
east, a sheer wall of wet rock plummets down into the troubled waters.
name: The River Anduin beside the Carrock

desc:
The river widens again after having run past the lone high rock to the north.
The stream is still swift, and the air is filled with a vapour of water. Lush
plains on the western bank slope upwards steeply, hiding the tops of the Misty
Mountains from view. To the east, small hillocks rise out of the sloping ground,
higher and higher the further from the river.
name: The River Anduin

desc:
As the land approaches the course of two water streams, the grasslands of this
area have become more dense and less sparse. To the east are many thick patches
of grass and shrubs as you draw near the Anduin River. The land almost resembles
a carpet of grassland similar to the terrain to the west. Further to the south
lie more plains of small weeds that can be seen sprouting up where the ground
has ruptured in many places.
name: A Narrowing Expanse of Land

desc:
The ground is higher here and provides a good vantage point for viewing the
surrounding plains. A small but swiftly flowing stream runs at the bottom of the
valley north and west of here, it's water seems to support the lush vegetation
that contrasts with the otherwise barren lands around it. A tall and majestic
lonely oak grows atop the hill, it's crown shattered by a strike of lightning.
On closer examination, several very deep and scary-looking claw marks can be
seen in the thick bark of the old tree.
name: A Hill Overlooking the Valley

desc:
The stream leaves the valley bed here and flows eastward, towards the Anduin
that can already be seen from here. An unusual-looking lonely rock towers on the
other side of the river. A barely noticeable track follows the streamside
through the plains and heads straight up the hill to the west, atop of which a
lone lightning-struck oak tree stands.
name: A Bend in the Stream

desc:
The stream follows the valley rim and bends sharply eastwards. To the south, at
the top of the hill, a lonely and menacing, lightning-shattered tree towers
above the plains and sloping hills around. Remnants of old campsites suggest
that someone has been finding a shelter here in the not too distant past.
name: A Corner of the Valley

desc:
The ridge sharply breaks right north of here into a steep-walled valley, at the
bottom of which a narrow but turbulent stream flows its way eastwards, to
eventually join the River Anduin in the distance. The ridge continues on the
other side of it, separating the higher barren plateau from greener grasslands,
as it runs in almost straight line north-east through the vast plains.
name: The Ridge of a Valley

desc:
A peaceful stream, almost the size of a small river, cuts east-west through the
barren plain. The waterfront is lined with low bushes, about the only kind of
vegetation around as far as the eye can see. The relative calm of the stream
seems to be changing in a short distance to the east, where a cloud of vapour
hangs above the foamy waters. A loud rumbling sound can be heard from there, as
the water descends into a narrow gorge between steep cliff walls on both sides,
towering more than twenty feet above the flow.
name: A Stream through the Plains

desc:
As the grasslands reach to this area of the landscape, they scatter more
sparsely if you continue in all directions. Walking further east will only lead
you into a valley rich in vegetation and plant life. Looking further east is a
flowing river large in length and abundant in water. The landscape here is
rather uneven and has a definite slope down to the east.
name: High Grasslands entering a Valley

desc:
The surrounding land is occupied by open, steppe-like vegetation of perennial
bunch grasses, with flowering culms that rise several yards high. Most growth
and flowering occurs during late spring and early summer. The plants are dormant
in winter and mostly dormant in late summer and fall. The Great River Anduin can
be seen in the distance to the east. More fields of grass stretch outwards to
the west and a large area of plains to the south.
name: High Grasslands

desc:
There are some small patches of grass extending from the northern fields. If you
stare off to the west you can see the remains of a small hut. Everything else
around is very lifeless and mostly plains. Travelling to the east or south would
be more manageable due to the somewhat flat terrain heading off in that
direction.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The ground here is very unstable and will give way at any time if one were to
stand around for a long period of time. Navigating your way down the cliff will
only lead to injury if not well experienced in climbing high cliffs. The narrow
ridge seems to continue along in the north direction and looks less treacherous
and more stable the further north you go.
name: Treacherous Cliff along a Steep Ridge

desc:
The ground here is more stable than it is to the south. There is a thick carpet
of grass all around you. To the west you can see more fields of wild plant life,
while to the east drops a steep cliff followed by a vast expanse of plains. You
can sense the grass shifting during every sudden movement, probably caused by
the many insects jumping around within the fields. Looking to the far distance
of the north you can spot an outline of a decaying house structure.
name: Grassy Fields along a Steep Ridge

desc:
With the land more stable around here than further south, an endless amount of
grassland has grown with the flourishing soil. Large carpets of flowers are
noticeable in this area, along with tall weeds and short grass patches. To the
north is a huge wooden fence, forming the shape of an animal pen, that surrounds
an old and decaying barn. The only access for getting inside that pen would be
to walk around to the front entrance which would take some effort considering
the descending hill before the entrance.
name: Grazing Fields along a Ridge on a Low Hill

desc:
The large pasture stretches for a few miles along each side with an extensive
and unbroken fence surrounding the pastured land. There is a gate to the north
that opens and allows access out of the barn's fenced pasture. What would seem
to have supported many cows once long ago is now home to only a few inhabiting
cows that remain living throughout the many years of neglect by their previous
owners. The soil still maintains some nutrients enabling some cow life to live
day after day, yet given some time as the years pass by and if continuing at the
same rate, will become lifeless and inanimate.
name: Desolate Cow Pasture

desc:
Following the ground closely, a distinguished path can be recognized leading
into what seems like a rundown old farm house to the east. Even though the size
of the property might appear to be rather large, the inhabitants of this
building have long since left the land to decay naturally. A cow pasture with a
large and extensive fence can be viewed to the south that has restricted the
movement of the remaining cattle, which survived throughout the years that have
passed.
name: Path Leading to a Ruined Farm House

desc:
Walking carefully on the damp terrain you notice your feet half submerged into
the earth. Given the slight slope of the small stream's shore bank, one could
easily slip and fall into the ever so peaceful water flow. Given time and age,
the landscape here could very well become a marshy swampland inhabited by moss
and fungi. The patches of grass beneath your feet are very limp and hung over,
caused by the nearby water source.
name: Damp Plains along a Small Stream

desc:
The hilly ground to the west gives way to sloping plains that seem to be rolling
for miles and miles towards the Anduin in the east. The vegetation is becoming
thinner, and only a few sparse pines remain from the forests further up the
hills. A narrow but fast-flowing stream is coming from the mountains and finds
its way towards the river, zigzagging through the grasslands.
name: A Stream in the Plains

desc:
A small rivulet follows the bottom of the valley and heads away from the rising
slope eastwards, atop of which an old decaying structure can be seen in the
distance. The large structure to the south looks rundown as if not taken care of
for several years. The south bank appears to have been cultivated in the past,
but now it is fast overgrowing with tall grass and young bushes.
name: A Stream in the Plains

desc:
Vegetation grows scarcely on the exposed surface of the plateau. Only stunted
scrub and small, twisted bushes with prickles all over can survive here,
half-hidden among the rocks. The ground is mostly ash-coloured, like the stone
that forms it. Many cracks and chinks run along its surface, although not wide
nor deep enough as to make a real obstacle to your advance. The plateau comes to
a sudden end to the east, as a steep slope leads into the fields below.
name: Barren Plateau

desc:
The plains are slowly rising towards the west, from lush riverside meadows into
higher and bleaker grasslands, exposed to cold winds from the Misty Mountains.
The slope is seemingly growing steeper still further down south, for in a couple
of miles it can be seen breaking into a ridge, separating the wide berth of
Anduin from the first foothills of mountains far away.
name: A Slope to the Plateau

desc:
A ridge, almost fifteen feet high, rises from the sea of waist-high grass. It is
crumbled in places, which makes it possible to ascend to the higher plateau
behind its edge. Boulders, apparently broken lose from the stony wall, lie
scattered around, surrounded by low and wind-bent bushes.
name: A Worn-Out Ridge

desc:
Western slopes begin to level here, with ground slowly descending towards a
majestic wide river, a further few hours' walk away. Although still exposed to
the elements, the landscape isn't quite as barren the closer one gets to the
Anduin. In addition to low bushes, some trees dot the land, and several groves
of trees that appear to be willows can be seen further east and south, lining
this bank of the River Anduin.
name: Grasslands

desc:
The western grasslands come to an abrupt halt here, with the ground falling
almost vertically for some fifteen feet towards the Anduin flowing directly east
of here. Southwards, the ground declines more smoothly and leads to a small,
willow-lined peninsula between the Anduin and a smaller river which is
apparently fed by many small mountain streams from the Misty Mountains. Across
the river, plains seem to go on forever.
name: Riverside Fields

desc:
A worn-out stony ridge runs through the grasslands, with rubble and broken
boulders crumbling from its steep face, making it possible to climb it with a
little care and some effort. Eastwards, gently sloping grasslands continue for
several miles, and terminate by a wide river.
name: A Crumbling Ridge

desc:
A gravelly film of grey dust slides along the worn surface of the ground, pushed
by a wind that seems to never stop blowing here. Particles of rock, and even
small pebbles, are carried away, swept by the air, until they crush into a
larger stone, or disappear into dark crevices. The sound of the grain grazing
the earth spreads all over the plateau, merging with the howl of the wind.
name: A Barren Plateau

desc:
A high, peculiar crag rises suddenly out of the uniform plateau. The softest
areas of rock have mostly disappeared, devoured by the wind. Other surfaces,
that seem harder, have somewhat withstood the erosion, and they show a better
condition. Thus, the outline looks sharpened and irregular, as if serrated. All
around the pinnacle, rocky fragments of all sizes lie scattered on the flat,
grey ground.
name: A Crag in the Middle of the Plateau

desc:
Small rises, standing a bit further north, partially shelter this area from the
lashing, icy wind. The ground looks rocky and barren, and the landscape rather
featureless, except for a number of little, weak trees, battered by the cold and
elements, that grow scattered around. But even so, only a few blackened, pointed
leaves still stick to their bare, greyish branches.
name: Barren Plateau

desc:
Approaching the edge of the plateau a section of the ground seems to have given
way, and melts into the lowland below. Observing along the split terrain, the
inner rock can be seen exposed to the light giving notice to its faint grey
surface deep within the crevice. The ground around you is absent of living plant
life and is overpowered by the many small boulders and scattered rocks that have
been naturally set by the earth's natural weather conditions.
name: Edge of the Plateau

desc:
Walking towards the slight incline of the nearing plateau, the ground can be
seen opening up its way to the top of an exposed landscape. The only sign of
vegetation is the plant growth hidden behind the many rock formations across the
plateau. Travelling further up the inclined land would give way to a lifeless
earth only inhabited by the inanimate rocks that occupy the land at arm's reach.
name: A Bare Incline to the Plateau

desc:
The land continues to rise creating a sloping ground, which makes walking more
tedious if one were to travel farther east through the landscape. Noticeable
from this distance is the large plateau to the east, which opens widely in the
distance not far ahead. A small stream can be seen heading in a southerly
fashion, but slightly hidden within the valley's edge.
name: Rising Plains entering the Plateau

desc:
The terrain here can be seen ascending northward towards the dry plateau that
lies in the distance. There are noticeable amounts of dotted grass formations
buried on the surface of the many rocks that lie around in the surrounding area.
Some of the rocks probably fell from the rocky wall to the east. The wall is
mostly stone, but there are sections of earth from which vegetation sprouts. A
small stream lies in the distance to the south and can be seen flowing further
into the south.
name: At the Foot of the Plateau

desc:
As bare as the ground may seem, many of the protruding rock formations rising
from the ground beneath you could cause injury if a misplaced step was taken.
The rocky walls of the empty plateau can be seen, plummeting down to the ground
below. In rainy conditions small waterfalls can be seen forming, caused by the
small brooks that slide down the cliff-face. In the nearby landscape, some form
of grass formation can be seen but yet not abundant enough to create a field.
name: Edge of the Barren Plateau

desc:
The plateau comes to an end here, except for a ledge projecting southwards and
westwards. The dry weather and lack of water has caused the ground to harden up
and become extremely manageable to walk on. Overlooking in the westerly
direction, the rock face descends steeply into the sloping terrain. The
occasional cracking of a rock flying off the cliff to hit the ground breaks the
sound of the winds that sometimes howl around this cliff. Further out into the
distance, a large land of grasslands can be seen from above.
name: Corner of the Plateau

desc:
The land is full of lush and lively grasses that the water has helped to grow
for many years now. There are numerous rock formations in the surrounding
landscape which have probably fallen from the nearby plateau over time. The
impressive cliff-like face of the plateau stands majestically to the east, as if
it had been rooted out of the ground. Dew is common in the mornings when the
shadow of the plateau shrouds the land in darkness until the sun is high in the
sky.
name: Steeply-sloping Ground

desc:
The stream seems to bend here going eastwards cutting through the plains and
flowing at a faster pace as it heads further east. Even though the current seems
rather peaceful, you can feel the subtle nudges it gives off as the wind assists
its rate of flow. This small river can be seen leading into a rather great river
in the distance. Grassy plains can be seen to the western direction and to the
south.
name: Bend in a Stream through the Plains

desc:
The earth is more fertile here since it is sitting next to a lively stream. If
given the proper care some plant life can grow in these parts of the land. The
stream to the east seems to be flowing in a south- easterly direction. A
decaying barn surrounded by a large and high fence can be seen to the north
western region.
name: Fertile Plains

desc:
A decaying barn obstructs your passage to the west. The only means of passage is
to go around the large fence surrounding the old barn to the south or by
swimming through the nearby stream to the north. The land here is very fertile
and gains its nutrients from the lively stream to the east and north.
name: Fertile Plains

desc:
The stream continues to travel in a southerly direction, bending to the east
overflowing into a larger and slightly faster-paced stream. Small movements can
be seen under the watery surface indicating signs of fish life up ahead.
Continuing to journey along the stream's route will only lead you to greater and
much faster flowing water, which only the most experienced swimmer could
navigate through.
name: A Stream in the Plains

desc:
An old building sits lonely on a flat plain overlooking the river, with its
straw roof fallen in and crumbling chimney pointing to the sky above. Its
decaying walls are slowly being overtaken by grass and vines. The last remaining
doors and window shutters are still somewhat intact but the hinges squeak as the
wind blows through them. The pasture behind the farm still contains what little
life there is. Whoever once lived here must have left a long time ago.
name: Ruins of an Old Farm

desc:
This slowly decaying cow pasture is contained by a large fence and has a broken
down barn door in it. The fence blocks your path in more than one direction,
which constrains your movement. There are only a few cows still left standing
about that have been able to live off the still depleting nutrients that exist
within the soil in the pasture. Given some time this pasture will be as lifeless
and inanimate as the rest of the farm.
name: Desolate Cow Pasture

desc:
The scenery along the landscape is very peaceful and soothing from this point of
view. An old decaying farm house lies in front of you to the north, with a large
wooden fence obstructing your passage. The only means of getting into the barn
would be to walk around the wooden fence and through the front entrance.
However, going for a closer look within the holding grounds of the barn and
looking for any living inhabitants would prove a waste of time since they seem
to have left it to decay many years ago.
name: Flat Plains

desc:
The area around you portrays a very quiet and calm place. The only thing which
disturbs the silent plains is the sound of wind blowing by your ears. When you
look around you all that can be seen are plains in most of the directions. To
the west lie some fields. To the north can be seen a large fence surrounding a
decaying barn.
name: Lowland Plains

desc:
The soil around you is very poor in nutrients which makes the ground flat and
rugged. It is very dull in the area about you and travelling further east would
only lead you to more dull plains. The only presence of life is more to the west
where fields of grasslands can be seen nearby. To the north is a subtle faint
impression of a decaying barn surrounded by a large fence.
name: Blunt Plains

desc:
Travelling through these lifeless plains is easier without the resistance of
grass and plant life in your way. Walking in the western direction would only
lead you into fields and fields of grass, whereas going in any other direction
would only lead you to more plains.
name: Dull Plains

desc:
The grassland area that extends from the north and eastern direction seems to
fade away as you glance farther south. More plains lie to the west and they seem
to have their ability to grow plants. While a small river formation can be
remotely seen in the southern distance, a greater river lies to the east.
name: Temperate Plains

desc:
This area remains grassland, and is not taken over by woody shrubs and trees,
because of seasonal drought, occasional fires, and grazing by large mammals. The
soil of these grasslands is the deepest and most fertile in the surrounding
area. To the east is a great river that is rich in colour and pleasant to more
viewers.
name: Fescue Grassland Plains

desc:
The grass here is a type of native grass, which is tough enough to withstand the
strong dry winds of both summer and winter. The presence of these grasslands in
this area allows for over wintering of large herds of elk. To the eastern
direction lies a great river of tremendous power and strength. In every other
direction lie more grasslands.
name: Fescue Grasslands

desc:
The native grass here extends from the west and covers the ground beneath you.
The cover of these grasslands is high enough to support different types of deer,
elk and similar wildlife inhabitants. In the fescue grassland region further
east though, the elk are generally absent due to the lack of nearby forest
cover. Directly to the north of you is a bend in a stream heading in the eastern
direction.
name: Fescue Grasslands near a Stream

desc:
An abundant amount of grass can be seen beneath your feet in all directions,
continuing more densely in the north and east direction, and less sparse in the
southern lands. As you stray farther to the north-east, the great Anduin River
can be reached within arm's reach. Besides the grasses that grow here,
wildflowers every spring provide a beautiful display of colour.
name: High Grasslands

desc:
The grass here is richer and can be seen more numerous as you peer to the north
and to the east direction. A great river can be seen over in the east, which
flows in a north and south route. Far to the west more plains cover the ground,
similar to the view from the south.
name: Temperate Grasslands

desc:
Immediately walking over the ground beneath you gives a sense that the land
within arm's reach was manipulated by humanoid-like beings. Although no strong
signs of cultivation are present, there is a noticeable trace of heavy movement
throughout the ground around you. Whether this is an indication of life forms
that once lived nearby long ago, it is too difficult to determine the exact
details of these living forms.
name: Deserted Plains

desc:
The endless fields of plants and flowers come to a complete stop here as the
hill slopes downward at a rate which only an experienced climber would be able
to navigate. The only way down this steep ridge is to the east and south where
sharp protruding rocks can be seen sticking out of the sharply inclined hill.
From this vantage point, fields of grasslands can be seen to the east and are
continuous to the surrounding landscape.
name: Bounteous Fields along a Steep Ridge

desc:
A sensation of life and activity comes from the boundless number of flowers
growing within the grasslands all around you. A rainbow of colourful flowers can
be seen within arm's reach and extends all around you creating a bright bed of
flowers. Only time passing will stop the growth of these grasslands, as the hot
season comes to an end and the cold season begins. The land descends and reaches
the plains below further along the south and to the east.
name: Fertile Plains on a Hill

desc:
The land heading towards the southern and western directions seems to be more
intact than any other of the ruined fields nearby. Most likely, the surrounding
area to the south and west endured more maintenance, considering it was able to
preserve the life that thrives to grow to this present day. Perhaps some manlike
being assisted with the vegetation that flourishes in this area allowing small
animals to feed off and run wild about.
name: Lush Plains on a Hill

desc:
There are only small portions of loose grass remaining in the surrounding
landscape. Observing more closely, signs of heavy passage can be seen crossing
the ground beneath you in all directions. All that remains is the permanent
damage done to the flourishing grasslands that once encompassed the land long
ago. There is a large fence preventing your travel to the east and the only
access to get inside that pen would be to walk around to the front entrance
which would take some effort considering the descending hill before the
entrance.
name: Decaying Fields on a Hill

desc:
The landscape here descends down into the vast plains below. Travelling towards
the plains below will only lead to a task that requires some work of a skilled
climber. To the east is a ruined barn with a large wooden fence surrounding it.
A small stream can be seen from the top of this hill and can be followed with
the eye, flowing to the south-eastern direction. Further to the south the hills
become more ridged and harder to travel along the terrain.
name: Decaying Fields on a Low Hill

desc:
A mist of water can be felt coming from the northern direction. The ground
beneath you seems to sink in about half an inch as the spray settles on the
floor and becomes absorbed by the soil, turning into wet earth. Badly decaying
fields of grassland can be seen from the horizon and a sense of warfare can be
felt in the frigid wind blowing around you. Travelling in the south-westerly
direction can only satisfy one's curiosity of the remains left behind of a
battle taken place many years ago.
name: Soggy Plains along a Small Stream

desc:
As the ground drops the rapids become a series of small falls and streams,
throwing mist and vapour into the air as the water finds its way among the
slopes and steps. A few of the falls are several feet high, but by being careful
enough you can probably ford the stream with little or no trouble. Trees are
becoming sparser when you move eastwards, where only vast sloping plains and
grasslands extend as far as your eye can see.
name: Cascades

desc:
Although the stream through these parts is rather small, the water flows
extremely fast. The current is strong enough to easily knock a man off his feet
and carry him downstream. Flowing with determination towards the eastern rim of
the large vale, it's clear to assume these rapids will result in a cascade of
small waterfalls and streams farther away to the east. To the south is a vast
expanse of battle-scarred plains.
name: Small Rapids

desc:
The ravages of war are seldom more evident then they are upon this place. Soil
rich with the blood of fallen warriors has only recently healed enough to allow
new growth. A few weeds have sprouted up through the shallow graves of limbs
lost by those who fell in battle.
name: Fields of Sorrow

desc:
The sound of the wind sweeping across this old field could easily be mistaken
for the howl of a dying man. Everything about this place conjures up the memory
of death. Soil rich from soaking up the bloodshed of a great battle also holds
the broken bones and mangled armour of those bodies left to rot without so much
as being placed in a shallow grave. Since that time a few straggling weeds have
managed to take seed and begin to heal this once fertile land.
name: Fields of Sorrow

desc:
Despite the vast destruction of the surrounding areas, this flat parcel of land
seems to have been for the most part spared. Bushes and even a few groups of
young trees grow upon the sparsely grass-covered ground. However, this place is
not without its scars. While it doesn't appear that armies clashed on this spot,
the footfalls of troops passing by have left some patches of ground notably
bare.
name: Surviving Fields

desc:
The dark soil is rich with nutrients, allowing the plant life here to flourish.
In the spring, beautiful wild flowers grow in abundance, displaying a dazzling
variety of colours and scents. The grass grows thick and hardily, providing
ample cushioning to anyone walking upon it. Small animals wander about, munching
on berries or chewing on stalks.
name: Fertile Plains

desc:
Despite the destruction that raged upon the land to the west, these plains are
quite fertile and filled with life. Small animals dart around from time to time
scavenging for food or nesting materials. Wild grasses and berry-bearing shrubs
complete the scene of natural harmony.
name: Fertile Plains

desc:
Before you lies a vast field of flowering plants. During the spring the flowers
blossom, creating the perfect habitat for colourful butterflies and pollen bees.
In the fall when the flowers are gone their seeds will take root, the beginning
of the following year's floral display.
name: Budding Plains

desc:
The remains of a small hut still stand here exposed to the elements. Looking at
the front part of the ruined room the lack of life here is apparent. Even the
spider's web high in one of the corners has been abandoned by its eight-legged
owner. Further inside is a more startling sight; lying on the floor is what is
left of what one can only assume was this hut's owner.
name: Remains of a Hut

desc:
Even though these great fields appear to be quite an hospitable place for all
kinds of animals, none that roam this land seem to be indigenous to this region.
Closely examining the terrain one notices a small hut far to the east and a
small river to the south slowly flowing west to east.
name: Silent Plains

desc:
This land has been clearly scorched by a horrible fire. Nothing escaped the
searing heat. Those plants and grasses that were not completely consumed are
withered from their proximity to the flames. Fine soot carried by the wind
across the plains leaves a layer of black ash over everything. To the south one
can catch a glimpse of fertile lands, but northward the destruction is even more
widespread.
name: Charred Plains

desc:
Scorched by the deadly flames of what may have been a careless wildfire or one
set in the ravages of war these plains are almost completely lifeless. Charred
remains of what could have been a young tree lie as a lone sign that this area
might once have been home to several varieties of vegetation.
name: Blighted Landscape

desc:
A few plants struggle to survive in this environment, crippled by a lack of
direct sun and scorched earth. Some dusty grey flakes of ash, spread over a
large portion of the desolate land, give evidence of perhaps a wild or
intentionally set fire. Robbed of nutrients, not yet replaced by the natural
order of things, the few plants present will soon fade.
name: Blighted Landscape

desc:
In the spring when the sun smiles upon this vast plain this fertile land bursts
with flowers. A hardy variety of plant, the blooms last all through the summer
months. In the fall, when all their petals have dried and fallen to the ground,
this area gives off a sweet smell. In the winter, there is very little growth,
while the bulbs of those flowering plants safely nestled in the soil are waiting
for the cycle to begin again.
name: Dazzling Fields

desc:
The plants here have never recovered from the trampling of armies during what
must have been a great battle. The once blood-stained earth still carries the
broken bones and battered armour of the fallen warriors. In some places the
fletching of an arrow or the hilt of a sword can be seen protruding from the
ground.
name: Fields of Sorrow

desc:
It is hard to imagine this quiet spot was once the site of a great battle. The
soft soil, home to a variety of weeds and grasses, almost obscures the remnants
of bones that lay as silent markers to those who died and were not even buried.
name: Sleeping Meadows

desc:
The stream straightens out here and picks up speed on its journey eastward.
Still quite calm, it emits a pleasant transparent sound as it passes the meadows
that grace its shores. Some small birds flutter about bathing in the water and
drinking from the cool, clear stream. Some other birds along the banks sing
sweet songs, as if to thank the gods for placing this stream upon Arda.
name: Small Rapids

desc:
This small trickling stream winds through the plains. The water is crystal clear
and flows quickly, inviting weary visitors to take a dip. As the stream slowly
wanders back and forth on its way eastward towards the Anduin River it produces
a small tinkling sound. Small animals come and go, stopping only to drink from
the banks of the river.
name: Small Stream

desc:
Almost utterly silent is the meadow that unfolds upon this land; the only sound
to be heard is the whisper of a small stream. The stream speaks with nature's
dulcet voice trying to maintain harmony, lest other less peaceful sounds would
try to overwhelm this quiet place.
name: Sleeping Meadows

desc:
The land gradually slopes to the west. At dusk when the wind blows it creates
the illusion of these winsome grasses bending to pay homage to the setting sun.
This entire area seems to carry an air of simplistic calm. Each morning these
tranquil plains seem to awaken with life.
name: Slanting Plains

desc:
Piles of stone and debris lie scattered across these barren plains. Just south
of here all passage is blocked by the remnants of an old stone wall. Perhaps
once quite sturdy, the wall is now slowly crumbling to dust, leaving only rocky
fragments and boulders as a trace of its former glory.
name: Plains outside the Walls

desc:
The vast plain stretches out in all directions except to the west. There the
remains of an old stone wall blocks passage for animals as well as plants. The
worn, broken rocks that make up the wall are covered in vines and ivy, which are
slowly and inevitably choking the stone, assisting the elements in breaking the
wall down into no more than rocky rubble.
name: Outside the Walls

desc:
The faint odour of rotten herbs lingers in the air. Almost all of the plants
sticking up out of the ground are dead and decaying. Most of the leaves have
fallen off, although a desperate few still cling to stalks and branches. Some
small trees stand here, malnourished and slumping, their boughs touching the
ground. Even the few blackened vines that once grew with vigour have given up
their attempts to scale the ruins of the village walls.
name: A Garden of Rotting Herbs

desc:
Set a bit lower than the surrounding plains land, this quiet old field has
remained untouched by person or animal for what could have been an eternity. The
ground is no more than dirt; not even weeds have laid seed. In fact, there is
very little evidence anything ever grew upon this spot. Even the wind seems to
overlook this place, blowing high above the ground.
name: Silent Plains

desc:
Damp and silent is the garment worn by this abandoned ground. No being of
conscience could possibly desire to remain upon this dismal place for longer
than necessity demands. All that can be seen across the barren landscape is land
raped by fire. To the east the plains are far flourishing, yet from here they
too are silent. To the south-west the grasping claws of a rushing river can be
heard, seemingly yearning for someone or something to snatch from the riverbank.
name: Barren Plains

desc:
The northern wall of this house is severely damaged, having buckled under the
weight of the roof. The north section of the roof has completely collapsed
leaving a sizeable hole above. Whatever caused the rampant destruction of the
village moved north from here leaving a large breach in the wall. Shattered
stones lie scattered all across the ground.
name: A Small Obliterated House

desc:
The remnants of an old stone wall stand sentinel here, preventing travel
southward from this point. In all other directions the plains stretch out toward
the horizon from the base of the wall creating a smooth, billowing grassy
expanse unbroken save for a tower far to the north-east.
name: Plains outside the Walls

desc:
Like a fair maiden's long soft hair, these plains gently flow to the rhythm of
the winds as they stretch out eastward. The subtle nature of their charm is not
even diminished by areas of heavily burnt grass and an imposing dark tower.
name: Fertile Plains

desc:
This entire area is filled with a remarkable stench. The pungent smell is
inescapable; not even fragrant mauve petals could dispel this foetid odour.
Progress through this area is impeded as one inhales this scent heavy air. The
source of the odours, which seems to be some rotting meat, is combined with the
stench of sulphur, creating an entire level of noxious vapours. Staying here for
any period of time would be unwise as who knows the damage inhaling these fumes
could cause.
name: Smelly Plains

desc:
A long time ago, when the deadly breath of war raged through this area, a group
of valiant warriors were surrounded and captured by an overwhelming force of the
Dark Lord. Warriors were decapitated after loosing arms and legs and some of
them were skinned alive. All that remains here now are piles of broken bones and
perhaps a few lost souls looking for vengeance.
name: Haunted Plains

desc:
Tortured by the plague of war, these fields have become a huge cemetery for the
fallen soldiers who fought here. The plants here have been trampled by the many
hordes who once walked over them; there is no new growth. The earth was once
made muddy, drenched in the blood of soldiers who fell in battle. Now the only
reminder is the dark colour and heavy consistency of the soil. Every so often
the fletching of an arrow or the hilt of a sword can be seen protruding from the
ground. As the wind sweeps across the field it creates a long, slow sound, like
the moaning of someone on the verge of death.
name: Fields of Sorrow

desc:
The vast grasslands seem to stretch out forever continuing on in every
direction. To the south the ground becomes rocky, and there are some buildings
far off in the distance. The fields to the east have a sombre look about them,
and the earth there is dark as if there were a shadow emanating from the ground.
An abandoned lodge lies in ruins just west of here.
name: Vast Plains

desc:
Large rocks seemingly sprung out of the ground make up this rocky landscape.
This area appears to be an extension of the Misty Mountains; perhaps this is the
very place at which they take root. The large boulders resemble a creature's
jagged maw, with rough grey teeth in a huge open jaw that could snap shut at any
moment. At times the light shining down from the sky creates a strange array of
shadows, and one must wonder if there are some monstrous creatures around. An
imaginative traveller might think of scenarios in which the boulders take on
lives of their own.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
As this land gradually blends itself into the Misty Mountains more and more
stones are visible in the soil. As one progresses from north to south the rocks
gradually decrease in size, from huge boulders to small stones. Although the
larger stones pose an obstacle to travellers who must traverse them, the medium
sized chunks of rock prove to be the biggest nuisance. They jut out of the
ground at the most inopportune times, and one could easily trip over them.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
This sprawling field sports an abundance of stout bushes and tall grasses, with
many small rocks embedded in the soil. Occasionally some small trees reach up
out of the bushes towards the sky. Small animals dart in and out of the bushes,
creating little ruffles in the grass as they run through. At times a lone hawk
or a flock of birds cross the sky.
name: Bushy Plains

desc:
The remains of a large old wall still stand here, blocking passage to the
south-east despite its crumbling state. The old wall follows a gentle curve,
making a circle that continues on to the east and south. Traces of debris and
piles of rock line its path.
name: Outside the Walls

desc:
Tall stone columns on either side of the road are all that is left intact of
this once lively village. The great gates, that once served as a border between
this city of Woodmen and the wilderness beyond, fell long ago. The city walls
are visibly weakened, with holes of varying sizes. Some of the holes are large
enough to allow medium-sized creatures to pass through. The gates themselves
appear to have taken quite a beating before they fell; there are large round
impact marks at about waist height, possibly from a battering ram. Splintered
fragments of the gate are scattered about the ground, and some larger chunks are
still bolted to the columns. Whatever force laid siege to this city, it must
have been quite massive.
name: Entrance to a Ruined Village

desc:
Just outside the old village walls this old field lies silently as a memory of
the old inhabitants of this area. A few wooden poles rise out of the ground, the
remains of a fence in a shape reminiscent of the grin of an old man that has
lost almost all his teeth. The field itself is slowly being reclaimed by the
surrounding plains, its man-sown crops fighting a valiant, but inevitably
losing, battle.
name: Ancient Fields

desc:
Not much light enters this room, if room is even the word to describe this
place. It is more of a dingy pit, hastily carved out of the earth, as evidenced
by the rough walls. Deep scratches and ruts along the earthen surfaces catch the
shadows created by the dim light, giving the illusion of fresh wounds. The air
is humid, suggesting that it might be a cellar of some sort, if it weren't for
the well-worn bones that litter the ground. Judging from the pervasive odour, it
is more likely a dungeon, or a holding pen for wild animals or pets.
name: Animal Pit

desc:
Looking around, it is evident that these are the barracks for the soldiers
stationed at this outpost. The overwhelming stench of sweat and rotten meat
hangs heavily in the air, seeming to emanate from no particular place. The odour
is in everything. Two-high bunks are lined up against the walls; most are quite
untidy, and the sheets hanging off of them are mottled with stains. The
splotches have varying degrees of darkness; some look like blood, while others
are more consistent with sweat and dirt. Old clothes are strewn about everywhere
on the floor. Both the clothes and the floor are stained with spots similar to
those on the sheets. It is evident that nothing here has been washed in a long
while, if ever. A heavy wrought iron grid is embedded in the middle of the
floor, with large hinges bolted to an adjacent floorboard. A large metal ring is
attached to the side opposite the hinges, likely a handle allowing it to swing
up, giving access to whatever lies below.
name: Barracks

desc:
Of all the places in this building this must be the most vital. Here the
guardians of the tower can gather information about enemy or friendly movements
from quite a distance so that they can send a messenger to prepare their
chieftain for whatever is coming. The tower's importance is evidenced by its
sturdy stone construction. Heavy blocks of stone hold the walls together by
weight and placement. A domed roof protects the room from the elements, while
small portals in the walls allow viewing of the surrounding lands.
name: In a Watchtower

desc:
This is a dark and humid room at the heart of the outpost with a large door made
of oak. The height of this room is approximately seven feet and like the rest of
the outpost the walls are made of roughly cut stones that just as well might
have been picked directly from the ground. It seems as if the guards of this
outpost used this as a storage room. The smell of half-rotten meat tickles your
senses.
name: Musty Room

desc:
This seems to be the entrance hall of a small outpost. The walls are made of
large, roughly cut stones. The craftsmanship is less than expert, and everything
about this outpost leads one to believe it was built hastily. This particular
building has not stood here long, judging from the age of its materials. The
place lies in squalor, evidence that the most recent occupants may have been the
orcs.
name: Fortress in the Plains

desc:
The ceiling of this tiny room is at most four feet high. The walls are made of
heavy stones, which have been laid on top of one another, held in place only by
weight. The only natural light in here enters through small cracks between the
stones. This room does not appear to be a storage space, but rather some type of
hidden escape route, should some enemy force come unexpectedly.
name: Backdoor

desc:
Completely water-filled, this small chamber was once part of the secret escape
route that led from the old fortress into safety. The water is stale and dark as
it is standing almost perfectly still. On the old floor of the chamber there are
the remains of old barrels and stands of chests. However, their contents have
long since become ruined and decayed.
name: A Flooded Escape Tunnel

desc:
This old tunnel was once dry and carefully maintained, but since water from the
nearby rivers has been eating away at the walls the entire route has been
completely flooded. The water here is stale and dark since no movement helps to
circulate it.
name: A Flooded Escape Tunnel

desc:
The small tunnel turns sharply southwards here but the water continues to drag
you slowly eastwards, towards the rocky walls. Small cracks in the wall allow
the water to pass through as it strives to reach the large rivers that lie
further to the east.
name: A Flooded Tunnel Bend

desc:
This is apparently the end of a small tunnel that isn't more than about four or
five feet high and not much wider. At one side of the tunnel there is a small
hole leading upwards. On the other side there is deep, almost impenetrable
darkness that is covering every sign of movement in the tunnel.
name: Flooded Underground Tunnel

desc:
This part of the tunnel is featureless and it runs from east to west. Despite
being carved out of the ground the walls, floor, and ceiling are quite smooth.
The dimensions here make the going quite tough for anyone either very tall or
rotund.
name: Flooded Underground Tunnel

desc:
This is one of the few inns in this part of the land; unfortunately there is not
much left of it. A large pile of broken wood scraps lies heaped in the middle of
the floor. There is a staircase at the eastern side of the inn, suggesting that
at some point there used to be a second floor to this building. The staircase
does not lead anywhere now, as the walls have been demolished, with the roof
collapsing in on top due to lack of support.
name: Ruined Inn

desc:
There are rumours of a great chieftain of the woodsmen, who lived in this area.
His exact residence was not certain, but it may have been this village. This old
lodge would most likely have been his dwelling. Not much is left intact, save a
large wooden bookshelf against the northern wall. Next to the eastern wall the
bed has taken heavy damage from an axe and now lies splintered and broken. A
shattered desk has been left heaped at the western wall.
name: Ruined Lodge

desc:
In the middle of a large carpet of shattered limestone stands a large altar made
of white marble, as evidence of the glorious chapel that once stood here. Now
the altar is the only remaining vestige of that chapel, for those things that do
not lie shattered on the ground among the limestone have probably been stolen or
burnt.
name: Ruined Chapel

desc:
Apart from the surrounding ruins this old square has remained completely intact.
A large white fountain containing two remarkable statues at its centre is the
most noticeable of this courtyard's features. One of the statues depicts a huge
bear standing upright. The other one is of a large man, in ranger's dress,
wielding a beautifully carved longbow.
name: An Old Square

desc:
Pieces of shattered and charred rock lie scattered all over the ground, mixed
with piles of debris and burned wood. What was once a house is now a mere pile
of rubble, all former markings and traces of architecture obliterated by some
great force.
name: Ruins of a House

desc:
A belt of oaks, old and leafy, stands to the east, and two rows of trees start
there, flanking a path that comes into the small hills in this area. The trail
winds through flower plots covering the soft slopes amongst the shade of the
tree tops. To the south and west, the rolling ground seems to stretch out, but
the path bends northwards and disappears wandering into the flower expanses.
name: A Path among the Flower-covered Hillocks

desc:
The path runs through small, square flower patches that have been carefully
planted around it, taking the shape of a large, tessellated expanse, that shines
full of colour. The diverse scents of the many different flowers mix together
forming a unique smell that is full of vaguely distinguishable, yet fine
details. The path bends here, leading westwards into the grassy hills, and
disappears into the flower plots to the south.
name: A Path through the Patches

desc:
In this land of countless vales, the miles seem to get longer under your feet,
demanding an additional effort to cover them. From the top of a hill, the
landscape around rolls in an endless series of hills and valleys, like the
roughed ocean during the most terrible storm. But the path, unaware of whatever
happens all around, continues its way winding through the hills, always flanked
by the rows of elms and oaks.
name: Up the Slope and Down the Vale

desc:
An east-west path is here, flanked by rows of tall oaks and leafy elms that have
been carefully planted at the borders of the trail. To the west, it seems to
head for a great rock that towers above the ground, silent and impressive,
while, in the other direction, the path, always in the shade of the tree tops,
goes into the hills and disappears among them. But you cannot discern any sign
of where it may lead to.
name: A Path Surrounded by Trees

desc:
A ford of huge, flat stones leads to the foot of a great rock, almost a hill of
stone. It crops out of the ground to the west, right in the path of the Great
Anduin, like a last outpost of the distant Misty Mountains, or a huge piece cast
miles into the plains by some giant among giants. At the base of the hill, a
comfortable-looking cave opens into the rock, while some well worn steps, carved
out of the stone, lead to the top. Certainly, this natural monument must have a
special meaning for the inhabitants of these lands, for a path starts at the
bank, and, flanked by rows of elms and oaks, disappears into the hills and vales
to the east.
name: A Path Starting at the Foot of the Carrock

desc:
Even though countless vales and hills follow each other in this area, from time
to time one draws your attention, as in this case. This particular dale appears
to be much deeper than the rest, for, although the hillocks flanking it are not
too high, it roams along a hollow in the ground. The hillsides tumble
majestically downwards to a dried river bed, a long-forgotten tributary of the
Anduin. To the south, the hillsides seem to lean towards each other, as if
trying to conceal what lies through the gorge between their craggy summits.
name: A Valley among the Hills

desc:
From the small hillocks that stand to the east, the ground comes tumbling down
to this majestic river bank. Further east and south, it appears to roll more and
more. There, at the top of a hill, an old wooden building seems to invite
travellers to take a rest. In contrast, a huge green expanse spreads all over
the countryside to the northeast, like a gigantic-sized grassy carpet that
perhaps extends to the boundaries of the dark Mirkwood forest, far to the east.
To the west, the river flows slowly southbound, with the imposing backdrop of
the Misty Mountain peaks behind it.
name: Low Hillocks near the River Shore

desc:
The flow of the Great Anduin, after detouring the huge rock that stands in its
path to the northwest, runs into this bank. Through the years, it has broken the
rock up into tiny stones, smooth and oval, that cover the ground by the
riverside like a cold, stony carpet. The blow of the wind raises small waves
that come to die on this fluvial beach, under the watchful look of the Misty
Mountain peaks.
name: Small Stony River Beach

desc:
A small, almost square flat area at the top of the stone provides a perfect
vantage point for observing the surroundings. The path leading down to the foot
of the hill appears very well trodden indeed, and leaves no doubt that this
place has been visited often over the years.
name: Atop the Carrock

desc:
In this area of the eastern bank, the slow but unstoppable flow of the powerful
Anduin has eroded the rocks that make up the ground. Through the centuries, the
waters have sometimes smoothed their edges, at other times they have broken them
up into small parts that lie scattered throughout the bank. The ground, although
stony, is covered in rounded boulders, thus making your advance less arduous
than might be expected.
name: Stony Bank

desc:
A low hillock stands to the east, with its slopes rising over the surface of the
waters of the Great River. These waters detour the contours, glide past and then
bend gently southwards towards the sea. The flow of the water is slow, but also
restless and mighty, because of the width of the river, that, in this flat and
uneven area, sometimes reaches the mile.
name: Bend in the Anduin River

desc:
The largest river in Middle-earth advances steadily southbound, to meet the salt
waters of the still very distant Belfalas Bay. Its water, proceeding from the
icy basins of the Grey Mountains, keeps cold to the touch for the whole year.
Its surface is always limpid and clear, like a window through which the Great
Vala Ulmo, Lord of the Sea, watches the land. For even here, many miles inland,
among dense forests and high mountains, it is Ulmo who keeps control of every
little pool and tiny brook.
name: The Meandering Anduin River

desc:
This low hill stands right in the path of the Great Anduin, forcing the waters
to pass by on along its western side. Throughout the years, the stream has
eroded the hillsides, shaping them into rocky upright walls, like small cliffs,
that rise several tens of feet above the waters. On the northern side, where the
stream falls directly upon the slope, the wear is more pronounced, and even from
the very edge of the cliff you cannot see its base. Beyond the river, the view
is excellent, as the landscape gradually ascends to the feet of the Misty
Mountains.
name: An Embankment over the Anduin

desc:
Countless rows of pikes exhibit the remains of unfortunate orkish heads and the
dried furs of the mountain wargs, like a grotesque hunting-trophy museum in the
open air. All sort of horrible grimaces show in the faces of those still
preserved heads, and they are even more dreadful in those half-rotten ones, let
alone the bare, sun-dried skulls, with their cadaverous eye sockets.
Undoubtedly, these remnants have been placed here for the benefit of other dark
minions. Grim warning of the destiny that awaits them, should they dare intrude
further into these lands.
name: War Trophies

desc:
Severally coloured flowers grow here and there among the grass. They add a dab
of colour to the dazzling, deep green expanse that covers the ground like a huge
carpet. To the east, the grass fields disappear, as small patches of carefully
planted flowers take their place, whimsically disposed in a very colourful
mosaic. A faint, yet clean buzzing seems to hover in that way.
name: Edge of the Flower Patches

desc:
Great patches of flowers, all the same kind growing together, cover these
fields. Especially there is clover: cockstomb or purple clover with waving
leaves, and small, white clover too, their little leaves swinging in the wind,
as if in time to the background buzzing. An intense, sweet, honey-like smell
impregnates the air, filling your senses.
name: Clover Fields

desc:
A smooth, long slope rises gently towards a hill to the west that, small as it
is, it still stands out of the plains around. The grass grows high and fresh on
the hillside, almost wildly, as if it had remained undisturbed for years.
Looking about, you can see the Great Anduin flow from the north. It flows past
the remnants of an old pier, disappears briefly behind the small hill, and then
continues its long way southbound.
name: Grassy Hillside

desc:
The wide river continues its way towards the sea, running through these vast
grasslands that extend between the snowy peaks of the Hithaeglir and the dark
and sinister Mirkwood forest. The flow avoids every little roughness of the
ground in an endless series of gentle bends. Here, the river runs into a small
hillock, and it turns westwards to detour it. But throughout the years, the
stream has worn away the hillside, and now it towers vertically over the cold
waters.
name: A Meander in the River

desc:
The simple beauty of these lands shows clearly in this riverbank. A gentle,
grass-covered embankment descends softly to the riverside, where the quiet and
silent waters seem to caress the little round stones on the ground as they flow
along. Now and again, a tiny wave comes to the shore, but it fades away before
breaking, as if it dared not making any noise that might disturb the quietness
of this spot.
name: Wet, Grassy Bank

desc:
A small, lonely hill stands to the east, like a lump on the ground that stands
out of the grassy plains around, and it is towards its top that this hillside
rises, gradually but steadily. As it ascends, some weeds and scrubs begin to
show among the grass, as if the latter ruled undisturbed in the low lands but
the other plants challenged its reign on high. To the northwest, at the river-
bank, you can see an old pier floating on the quiet waters of the Anduin.
name: Wild Slope

desc:
The half-rotten but still recognizable remains of an old landing stage float
mildly on the quiet waters of the river, along the eastern bank. Long ago, trade
was intense and prolific in these lands, but the declining of Gondor and the
strengthening of the orkish settlements in the mountains made an end of it. In
those days, small barges sailed the river, light enough to allow portaging when
they reached the Anduin rapids, to the south. But those were better times, and
the fine goods from Gondor are now rarely seen this far north.
name: River by a Ruined Pier

desc:
For a moment, a heavy gloominess seems to engulf the river, as it runs along the
withered western bank, where everything looks wasted and desolated. The waters
seem to darken and turn cloudy and turbid, as if trying to conceal some ominous,
forbidden secret that may lie hidden on the river floor. And they even seem to
hasten, trying to leave this place behind as soon as possible.
name: A Mournful Section of the River

desc:
Slowly but steadily, the waters of the Anduin continue their long travel
southbound, having started many miles away at the icy springs in the Ered
Mithrin, in the northern vales. Here, the stream has flooded the eastern bank,
forming a small marsh where vegetation grows dense. On the contrary, a low
hillock stands on the western riverside, silent and mysterious, and somehow it
seems as if the own mighty Anduin avoided its slope.
name: The Great Anduin

desc:
The river once again stretches out on its way far to the south, the water
flowing slowly but purposefully towards the sea miles away. A cooling breeze
runs over the surface of the river, creating ripples and dancing on the water.
Along the shores can be seen small streams of rainwater adding to the might of
the river in its journey south. To the east the bank rises unto great, rolling
grasslands that stretch out as far as the eye can see. To the west the Misty
Mountains can be seen looming in the distance.
name: The Great River Anduin

desc:
Pouring down over the ledge of black rock, the great river falls a short
distance here. A cold wall of spray rises up from the chute. The seething
current is coursing around everywhere, throwing forward everything and anything
that gets in its way and pulling it back with a relentless force. If careless
enough, one might not break the pull of the river, and end up in the deep and
dark surface of the mighty flow.
name: A Strong Eddy in the River Anduin

desc:
The great river rises here in a swell of foam and spray as it sweeps over the
top of massive black rocks lying just beneath the surface of the water. The
jagged edges of the rocks have been worn down with the eternal flow of the
current, but they still churn the waters and make passage difficult, even for
the experienced boat pilot. A quick glance reveals that the eastern bank of the
river is lined with dark mud, while open grasslands hasten away in the distance.
name: Rocks in the River Anduin

desc:
The waters of the River Anduin seem dark and forbidding and give away no hint of
what lies deep beneath the surface. The river is vast, the large body of water
flowing at a stately pace towards the distant south. Small fish can be seen just
beneath the surface, trying to catch a glimpse of the sunlight. To the far west
the peaks of the Misty Mountains loom, and to the east vast, rolling plains
stretch out.
name: The Mighty River Anduin

desc:
The central channels of the River Anduin sweep by, silent and grey, leaving only
subtle markings on the surface that belie the power of the swift under- current.
Schools of fish can be seen feeding in the warmer currents near the surface,
while the murky depths of the Anduin hide the passage of the larger predators.
White against the distant sky, the lofty peaks of the Hithaeglir rise to the
west, while to the east the rocky shore melts into an endless plain of rolling
grasslands.
name: The Mighty River Anduin

desc:
The grey surface of the water slides by swiftly, a swirling pattern of whirls
and ripples catching and reflecting the light. An occasional branch or bole of
some mighty tree drifts by, soaked and half-submerged in the powerful flow. The
distant shorelines are lined with tall and hoary willows, their grey roots
twisting down the low banks. The western horizon is sharply dominated by the
majestic peaks of the Misty Mountains, while rolling grasslands stretch away
eastwards toward the distant and unseen eaves of Mirkwood.
name: The Mighty River Anduin

desc:
This field of grass and wildflowers lies directly west of the great river
Anduin. The shores are low and sandy, making a perfect place to launch a vessel
into the river, should one attempt to cross. The great river would make any
attempt to wade or ford it impossible because it is just too wide. Looking to
the west, you spy in the distance the great Misty Mountains, while the eastern
banks of the river are lined with stands of willows.
name: A Grassy Riverbank

desc:
Long ago, after a glorious but painful victory against the dark minions, the men
of the northern vales dug out a barrow for their fallen people. The Nenedain, as
the elves call them, chose this small hill near the bank. It was hollowed out of
the very earth, and a huge stone was set to block the way in. Nowadays, the tomb
remains intact. The grass grows green and fresh over the hillock, but, strangely
enough, there is no sign of animal life nearby, as if some unknown power scared
the beasts. Perhaps it is the same power that has protected the place from
plundering.
name: An Old Barrow

desc:
The crudeness of an old battle, waged countless moons ago, has forever set its
indelible mark in what once was a beautiful riverside. The ground is scrambled
and spoiled; dense, black, dried stains fill the bottom of the holes among the
wasted earth, and desolation extends wherever you look. Everything seems still
and quiet, almost frozen, but sometimes the clashing of swords, the screams of
fighting men and the howls of orcs and wargs seem to come back from the past
like a soft, almost inaudible echo, rising from the ground itself.
name: Wasted Riverbank

desc:
A huge, coal-black stain is all that remains of a one-time green and fresh
riverbank. The grass, that once grew high and dense, has been burnt to the root
and blackened ashes have taken its place in the ground. Now and again a slight
glint catches your eye, perhaps some metal that didn't melt down, or maybe the
dried remnants of orkish blood, for this desolation is the result of a huge
pyre. The men of these lands, though kind and hospitable to their kin, hate orcs
and wargs beyond imagination and they are cruel and merciless to their enemies.
name: Burnt Riverside

desc:
A short flight of narrow and dusty steps, crudely carved out of the very ground,
descends into the hillock and opens into this square chamber with a low, rocky
ceiling. A great niche draws your attention to the rear wall, surrounded by a
coarsely-depicted but vivid battle picture. Below it, an inscription has been
engraved on the gravelly stone. Along both side walls many other smaller niches
fill most of the surfaces. The air is extremely dense and heavy inside the
chamber, and stale when you breathe it, as if it had remained still, waiting for
who knows how long.
name: A Chamber Hollowed out of the Earth

desc:
A wide spread of flowers fills this area, up to some old oaks planted to the
east, beyond which a high, thorny hedge hinders further advance. The bees
flutter restlessly around the flowers, looking for the sweet nectar they need to
produce their honey. The little ones make a faint buzzing sound, like a
background humming that rises and lowers in tone; but the larger bees drone
falteringly, as if joining every now and then up to the melody, and the whole
sounds like a natural orchestra that never stops playing.
name: Bee Pastures

desc:
Huge hives, most of them fastened to wooden sticks shining with beeswax, line up
row after row in this area of the grasslands. The smallest among them are
dome-shaped, and they have broad, circling rings along their surface, as well as
a little opening in their lower part, and there are a lot of this kind. But
there are also others much bigger that have been hollowed out of the earth, and
a huge hole opens into the inside, so large that it seems as if a giant tree had
been rooted out of the soil. An intense and sweet smell fills the air, while a
loud, unceasing drone hammers your ears.
name: The Outside Beehives

desc:
The unmistakable marks of trampled grass show outstanding on the ground: they
are certainly bear footprints, and, judging by their number, they belong to
several different beasts. They all are large and deep, but some are really huge,
and they slip a number of inches into the earth, the brutally squashed wisps
crowding at their sticky, deep green bottom. There are footsteps all around, but
they seem to lead north-south mainly.
name: Bear Tracks on the Grass

desc:
The loud buzzing becomes deafening as one walks through the numerous huge
honeycombs disposed in careful order, forming some kind of grid. They are big
sheets of honey and wax, divided into perfect hexagons, and very sticky, thus
hindering one's advance. The bees, on the contrary, come and go continuously,
fluttering all about, and gathering in dense swarms. Here, inside the hive,
nothing appears to be radically different from the bee pastures outside, but
everything intensifies: from the thunderous drone to the utterly penetrating,
almost wounding sickly-sweet smell.
name: Among the Honeycombs

desc:
A wide, humid grotto opens inside this hill. You can hear the noise of running
water coming from the northern wall. It flows in through the many cracks and
clefts, and accumulates in large pools, like small subterranean lakes, that
flood most of the cavern. At the back of the cave, to the south, the stones rise
over the pond, forming a rocky platform, and a broad passageway with damp and
earthy walls starts off there. Every now and then, large drops come down from
the ceiling, breaking the silence with a deep sound as they fall into the
dark-looking, shadowy lake.
name: A Half-flooded Cavern

desc:
These low, flat lands obviously hold better soil than the surrounding area, as
lonely, great beech trees grow here, stretching their branches towards the
distant skies. They look like great beings where they stand, keeping their
distance between themselves. In their shadow, low grass and a variety of flowers
and herbs grow. To the north and east, the great plains roll out, while to the
west the bank of the Anduin rises. To the south the trees stand closer.
name: Sparsely Forested Plains

desc:
This low rise is more exposed to the wind than the lands that lie about it, and
yet a good place for the living. Small trees grow about, casting shadows over
the grass and giving the rise a distinct air in comparison with the
surroundings. To the east the great plains roll out under the sky, while to the
west the great Anduin flows, like a tongue of steel in the green landscape.
name: A Sparsely Forested Rise

desc:
Here the banks of the Anduin resemble a marsh more than anything. In this soft
and moist soil some grey willows have placed their roots. Their long, thin
branches stretch out and hang to the ground, giving the place a sad and almost
ghost-like appearance. To the west lies the great Anduin, while to the east the
land rises unto great, rolling plains.
name: A Marshy Stand of Grey Willows

desc:
The waters of the Great Anduin have swamped the bank here, making use of the
earthy, rockless ground. A small marsh has formed, where vegetation grows thick.
The tall stalks of the reeds sprout out of the flooded ground, like a tiny but
dense forest that sometimes hinders your advance. The marsh seems to spread
southwards, and to the east, there is still vegetation to be seen, although much
more sparse compared to the other areas.
name: Rushy Marsh

desc:
The tall and strong stems of the reeds sprout out of the pools that cover the
ground everywhere. At times, they form little clusters, at other times they grow
in dense groups. The ground beneath your feet is soft, its solidity weakened by
the water that has flooded it. And yet it doesn't seem unsafe, as the roots keep
it compact. To the south, the earth becomes firmer and firmer, as high grass
takes the place of the reeds, and you can see a wooden structure floating on the
waters of the river.
name: Among the Reeds

desc:
The remnants of an old loading bay sway gently over the waters. In olden times,
this pier was used to unload goods that came from the northern vales, or even
the valuable loads from the distant Gondor in the south. But those were other
days, and now the quay has fallen into disuse. The wood is starting to rot away,
and a few planks fell off long ago. Some loose threads are all that remains of
the mooring ropes. A small outhouse, once used as storing area, crumbled long
ago into a pile of broken planks. Only the dock itself looks relatively
well-preserved, but even so, it doesn't give you any sort of confidence in its
stability.
name: Abandoned Jetty

desc:
A number of trees grow scattered on this gentle, grassy slope that rises very
