slowly towards the east away from the Anduin. Some of them stand lonely, as if
they had been expelled from the very earth by some unknown force, while others
group together in small clusters. The high, fresh grass crowds around their
trunks, as if trying to challenge them, sometimes concealing them by several
feet.
name: Some Dispersed Trees

desc:
The grasslands extend endlessly all around, and, from the distance, they seem to
tinge the ground with a thick layer of intense green dye. However, a few
features catch your eye: some trees grow scattered on the plain to the west, and
there are some to the north too, but they stand together there with a somewhat
dreadful look. In the opposite direction rises a small hillock with soft slopes,
behind which the meadowlands spread.
name: Grassy Fields

desc:
Certainly, water must be plentiful in this lands, because no matter what the
weather is like, the stems of the grass are always fresh and wet, and look full
of life. Sometimes, when the sun is shining high in the sky, the little water
drops sliding along their edges mirror the light, adding a diamond-like sparkle
to the deep green colour of the grass, and lightly tinting it with rainbow
coloured reflections.
name: Wet, Grassy Plains

desc:
A large pond has formed on this plain, surrounded by fresh mud soaked by the
cold waters of the Anduin. The stalks of the reeds sprout everywhere, along with
all sort of bushes and weeds that stick out of the mud and the pools. The pond
looks full of life; the background croaking of the frogs and the restless
singing of the crickets mix with the soft sound of the stems grazing in the
wind, only disturbed by the occasional hollow sound of a little beast diving
into the dark water.
name: A Miry Pond

desc:
The ground has become muddy in this mire, and only the filtered water prevents
it from drying. Even so, there are cracks and slits, and even furrow-like splits
running straight as in a ploughed field. The reeds, though not as numerous as in
the marsh to the west, line up along them like small, natural fences, as if
delimiting their own domains. Grey, dense mud fills the widest cracks, forming
small pools that seem to attract vegetation, as it sprouts thronging there.
name: Mudflats

desc:
A group of trees with twisted branches grow in this area of the meadowlands,
like a distant bastion of the dark and shadowy Mirkwood forest miles away to the
east. Their knotted trunks tower above the grass stalks, over which long
branches with dark, sharp-pointed leaves hang. Their dim, almost black tops form
a contrast with the intense green grass on the ground, casting large and
somewhat gloomy shadows upon it.
name: A Cluster of Trees

desc:
In the moist soil at the river's edge stands a cluster of weeping willows,
giving the landscape a beautiful, but also a sorrowful and desolate visage. As
the wind rustles the long, thin branches of the trees a strange music is born,
sounding as if the trees were impatient to move. Under the trees there is little
vegetation - all sunlight is absorbed by the willows' hungry leaves. Directly to
the west lies the river Anduin, and to the east, vast grassy plains.
name: Weeping Willows at the River's Edge

desc:
A seemingly ever-present wind rustles the grass of the wide plain, making it
sway and shift in all imaginable nuances of green and yellow. Occasionally, a
small cluster of flowers adds new colours to the landscape, breaking the
monotony. To the east, north and south the vast plains continue seemingly
unending, and to the west lies the great river Anduin. Beyond that, a hazy
bluish grey wall looms - the Misty Mountains.
name: A Grassy Plain

desc:
A great sward of long, golden grass climbs up this low rise amidst the
surrounding landscape of rolling plains. The few silhouettes of the lonely oak
trees or the moving shadow of a distant herd of deer can be seen dotting the
landscape and giving it life. An occasional breeze or gust of wind sweeps down
from the Misty Mountains lying far to the west across the steel-grey surface of
the mighty river Anduin. These open grasslands extend to the horizon in all
other directions, with the ground sloping gradually upwards in the north.
name: A Grassy Hillock

desc:
Green and yellow grass covers this even ground. The air is filled with a heavy
silence, broken with the occasional singing of birds and the whisper of the
wind. Broken blades of grass betray the presence of some animal activity.
name: Grassland

desc:
Amid the low grass grows a patch of colourful flowers. Apparently the flowers
are resilient and appear to bloom all year round - when one wilts, another
springs up. Some are dry already, some are just beginning to sprout.
name: Wild Flowers

desc:
The plains roll out endlessly under the great skies above you. With the great
variety of grass and low herbs, they seem to somehow reflect what is above. As
the wind reaches down and rustles the grass, the lands seem to wave in a strange
dance. The ground descends roughly to the west, reaching out to a riverbank.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
The tall grass covering these lands seems to shift beneath the skies in a never
ending dance with the wind. The colour of the grass is never constant as it
shifts with weather and rain, being a dark, dull grey when the weather is bad,
and a light, fresh green when blue skies are above. The grasslands stretch out
in all directions.
name: Grasslands

desc:
A shifting mass of grey, green and yellow grass lies all around, matching the
sky that lies above. Vibrant and full with little rushing creatures in the
summertime, dry and almost asleep in the colder months. Broken grass stems here
and there indicate the presence of a bigger beast passing through this area,
perhaps looking for smaller animals to chew on. The grasslands continue in all
directions.
name: Vast Grasslands

desc:
Rolling out under the skies, these vast grasslands seem to be unending. They are
home to a variety of different grasses, high and low, green and grey, young and
withered. Under the vault of the sky, the grasslands seem to match the heavens
as they stretch out. To the east the ground enters a small rise, offering a bit
higher view over the surrounding land.
name: Vast Grasslands

desc:
These flat grasslands seem vast and unending as they roll out under the skies.
They sport a variety of different grasses, in all thinkable shades of green and
grey. Some old, withered grasses add to the picture with their yellow, dried
leaves and stems. To the east the land rises towards more open fields. In the
other directions the plains stretch on, flowing like an endless sea.
name: Vast Grasslands

desc:
Thriving thick and healthy, the grass here grows high towards the sky. Thick
stems and healthy leaves in all shades of green rustle in the passing breezes.
Though quiet, the partly broken and stumped stems confirm the hiding wildlife in
this area. These grasslands continue in every direction while rising in the east
and revealing another endlessly waving plain.
name: Tall Grass

desc:
The tall, swaying grasses that stand here embrace all who enter this deep sea of
green. Looking around is not possible, except over the tallest grass, which
would reach to the shoulder of a grown man. To hide in these grasses would be
easy - just lying down would make one all but invisible. A beast might use such
an opportunity to stalk a traveller of the plains. To the north the ground
rises, bringing more grasslands into the view.
name: Tall Grass

desc:
The grasslands roll out endlessly, low rolling lands covered only with grass
that moves and shifts with the wind. As the grass moves, it shifts in colour,
from a solid green to a pale yellow or dull grey. The skies above seem to
reflect this as clouds come and go in an endless dance. The grasslands lower to
the south into the tall grass.
name: Rolling Grasslands

desc:
The low, rolling land is covered with high grass that reaches towards the sky,
only to be grabbed by the wind and sent into a strange dance, creating
mysterious patterns that change constantly. In the stillness, the only thing
heard is the wind and the grass rustling. The plains stretch out in all
directions.
name: Rolling Grasslands

desc:
This small hollow in the ground must have retained some water in the past,
allowing bigger plants to thrive. However, the bushes have dried out since then,
and all that is left are dry roots and sticks entangled with each other. Travel
becomes slightly difficult as the sharp ends of twigs catch both the clothes and
skin.
name: Dry Bushes and Sticks

desc:
A lively patch of grass grows here, between the ruins of an old fire to the
north, stony grounds to the west and dry bushes to the south. However,
underneath the fresh, green grass covering the ground, black residues of old
fire can be recognized. The even plains to the east seem to go on forever.
name: Grass

desc:
The grass only grows sporadically here, forming small clumps among pebbles and
rocks of different sizes. Since a steep rock wall towers to the west, it is not
difficult to guess the origin of all these stones. On the north, there are some
dark trees, and grasslands extend to the south and east.
name: Scattered Stones

desc:
An immaculate cover of grass extends to the horizon except in the west, where a
darker line may signify a change in the foliage. A careful observer might be
able to spot some small animals around, hurrying in their search for food and
shelter.
name: Grassland

desc:
On these vast grasslands the wind seems to be the only thing moving. But it also
moves the very grass itself, making it dance a strange and unfathomable dance.
Looking closer, one can notice small animals living in the tall grass that
covers these lands. Life is hidden, but it is there. The plains stretch out in
all directions.
name: Grassy Fields

desc:
The grass ripples when caressed by the wind, making strange patterns appear
everywhere. It resembles a sea, and similarly the life that exists, is seen deep
down and below the surface. Small animals scurry to and from their homes,
feeding their siblings and securing their little lives. The plains continue in
all directions, with an exception of a lowering slope to the south.
name: Grassy Fields

desc:
Here the grass stands very tall, reaching a man's chest. It seems to be of
another variety than the grass which covers most of these plains: the stems are
thicker, the blades longer and broader. Hiding in these grasses would be easy
since just lying down would make one all but invisible. The ground rises to the
north, bringing more grasslands into the sight.
name: Tall Grass

desc:
The tall grass continues, coming in from the north. The blades are long and
thick, reaching broadly out of the rich soil. As the wind comes in from the grey
river of Anduin, the grass springs and rustles, bending downwards in the mighty
breeze. Pawsteps of some bigger animal show that a beast of prey is stalking
these lands for its next meal. The grasslands continue in every direction,
rolling out like a giant colourful carpet.
name: Tall Grass

desc:
The plains stretch out here under the open sky as far as the eye can see, the
flat lands covered with high grass that shifts in the wind. Small animals scurry
between the grasses on the ground, almost invisible in the underbrush. As the
wind comes in from the north and west, the grasses shift and dance.
name: Open Plain

desc:
The grasses that cover this open plain are high and strong, their slim,
beautiful blades rustling and dancing in the wind. Below the grasses small
animals live, running as you approach. The grassy plains roll out in all
directions from here, stretching as far as the eye can see.
name: Open Plain

desc:
The plains stretch out endlessly, covered with grass that seems to dance with
the ever-present wind, creating strange and beautiful patterns. In this changing
pattern small animals live, their lives dominated by the search for whatever
food may be found here.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
Here the grasslands rise faintly from west to east, spilling out into the great,
rolling plains that lie between the Great River and Mirkwood. The rise is
covered with low grass, the only vegetation that can survive here on the
windswept rise. To the west flows the mighty Anduin.
name: Rising Grasslands

desc:
The riverbank rises sharply to the east, into the great plains that lie beyond.
The rise is constantly whipped by the winds that come in from the north and
west, over the grey waters of the Anduin, carrying cold moisture with them. The
only thing that grows here is low grass, and even that seems to be on the brink
of survival. To the south a group of small trees can be spotted, swinging their
branches in the breeze.
name: Rising Riverbank

desc:
The gentle slope of the eastern bank of the river is covered in lush grass and
tall stands of cattails swaying in the breeze. The river begins to broaden out
here in small, stagnant pools teeming with life. The marshy ground extends
further to the south, where a large stand of grey willow trees overshadows the
riverbank. Eastwards, the meadows sprawl out over gently rolling hilltops and
broad valleys.
name: At the Bank of the River Anduin

desc:
Here the land rises more steeply to the east, the slopes covered only with small
clusters of grass. The soil is dry and loose here, a result of many years of
slow erosion. The footing is unsafe, making it hard to climb the rise. To the
north the land ascends less steeply, while to the east the grasslands stretch
out beyond the rise.
name: A Small, Grassy Rise

desc:
This small, grassy meadow lies sheltered by low rises that ring this shallow
dell, untouched by the relentless winds. As a result, the land is covered with
small trees, lush rose bushes and herbs of all kinds. To the east the grasslands
continue, while to the west lies the riverbed of the mighty Anduin.
name: A Grassy Meadow

desc:
The grasslands begin here beyond the eastern bank of the river Anduin,
stretching on to the east. The slopes are covered by tall grasses that shift as
the wind comes in from the north and west, carrying with it a faint scent of
water. To the west, a stony slope leads to the top of a small rise by the
Anduin.
name: Rising Grasslands

desc:
Here the land rises from west to east, an extension of the riverbank of the
Anduin. The rise is covered by a variety of grasses that shift in the wind,
depicting at every time a new, unique pattern. Looking towards north, the
grasslands stretch out and another open plain enters the view to the east.
name: Rising Grasslands

desc:
The wind whistles as it passes over these low, grassy lands, playing upon the
grass as if it were an instrument. The sound created may not be called music,
yet it is fitting for these lands in a way. Under the great vault of the sky the
plains stretch out in all directions.
name: Rolling Grasslands

desc:
The plains roll out under the great skies, covered with grass that dances in the
wind, seeming almost a reflection of the skies. The colour of the grass shifts
with that of the sky, being a beautiful green when the sky is blue, and turning
a dull grey when the skies do. The plain stretches away from here in all
directions.
name: Rolling Grasslands

desc:
These low, rolling plains are covered with short grass that seems to shimmer as
the wind grabs the blades, joining in a never ending dance. Here and there,
ancient rocks lie about, smoothed over time by the caress of the wind. In all
directions the plains stretch out, and in the distant west you can spot the
great Anduin.
name: Rolling Grasslands

desc:
Silver blades of a foreign variety of grass grow tall on this rolling field.
Light striking the blades causes the whole field to somewhat shimmer and dance
in an almost magical fashion. The plains continue in all directions. Further to
the west, a small grassy hillock can be spotted, rising in the plains.
name: Grassy Fields

desc:
The ground evens out here; not even the slightest swelling can be seen. The
grass changes its colour in different spots: brownish here, reddish there and
yellowish at yet another part of the flat plain. A dark shape breaks the
straight line of the northern horizon.
name: Flat and Grassy Terrain

desc:
A massive rock rises from the surface of the surrounding grassland. It stands in
solitude among the seemingly endless plains. Small cracks have been carved into
the rock, looking somewhat like a natural stair. The grassy plains continue
rather unchanged in all directions. A few stones can be seen scattered to the
east.
name: At a Massive Rock

desc:
Grass grows thick and green here, leaving little room for anything else. It
bends slightly as you move through it. Eastward an outcrop of rock can be seen,
but in other directions smooth flow of grasslands are all that exists.
name: Grass

desc:
The steppe-like terrain continues, covered with grass and a few rickety bushes.
The undulating ground bulges here and there, but the terrain remains dirty-green
in all directions. Tracks of animals of different sizes lay scattered about the
terrain.
name: Grassy Terrain

desc:
Here the grasslands begin, stretching out to the east. The plains are covered
with tall grass that seems to dance with the gusts of wind. Sweeping over the
plains from time to time, the breezes fill the silence with the rustling of
grass, and at the same time emphasizing that very silence. The only part of the
landscape that intrudes upon this is the occasional cry of a lone bird high
above the plains.
name: Open Grasslands

desc:
The grasslands stretch out as far as the eye can see to the east. Here they
cover small rises and hills that have long since been worn down, the once sharp
rocks softened and dulled by the roots of small plants. Here one may find small
pockets shielded from the ever-present wind. Small, twisted trees and bushes
stand on these, forever opposing the environment in which they live.
name: Rolling Meadowlands

desc:
Here, shielded from the wind by the small hills to the north and the willows to
the west, a small field is covered by vibrant wildflowers. In an otherwise
unfriendly environment they manage to flourish, adding a welcome touch of colour
to the otherwise bleak landscape dominated by shades of green, yellow and grey.
To the west lies the Anduin, its eastern bank lined with willows, and to the
east the grasslands stretch out, seemingly endless.
name: A Field of Wildflowers

desc:
Amidst the lush reeds and grasses that flourish here, a stand of great hoary
willow trees line the soft bank of the Anduin. The long and slender tips of
narrow leaves fluttering in the breeze hang down, nearly touching the water. The
flat, blue-grey surface of the river extends far out westward, unbroken in its
breadth. Beyond the river, grassy plains quickly tumble into broken foothills
and then rise, soaring far above the plain in snow-capped peaks. Gentle
grasslands roll out as far as the eye can see towards the eastern horizon.
name: A Willow Stand at the Bank of the Anduin

desc:
Here the soil is too rocky to support any trees. Even the hard willows cannot
find enough soil to root themselves. The only vegetation consists of a few
flowers and some low bushes. The wind rushes in from the northwest, carrying
with it a scent of water and cool moisture. To the east the grasslands begin to
descend slightly, stretching out as far as the eye can see.
name: The East Bank of the River Anduin

desc:
Here the bank of the river rises slightly above the grey-blue surface of the
waters. On this rise, large boulders can be seen strewn in what almost resembles
a pattern of sorts. Maybe these shores were once someone's home, or perhaps
nature yet again plays us a trick. In the skies above, changes take place as
rain comes and goes, forever acting out a play that needs no spectators. To the
east, beyond a rocky incline, grasslands start to lower under the skies, meeting
a distant horizon. To the west, below, lies the great river Anduin and its
grey-blue waters.
name: A Low Promontory over the Anduin

desc:
In the stony earth no root has found soil enough to support it, yet some small
thorny bushes and an occasional flower stand here, defying the hostile
environment. An occasional spray of water is carried upon the wind from the
west, chilling all live creatures to the bone. To the east, the grasslands
stretch out, rolling towards a distant horizon.
name: The Rocky Bank of the River Anduin

desc:
Alder trees both young and ancient line the riverbank, their long and slender
branches reaching far out over the rippled surface of the water. A flat trail,
long since put to use, leads down into the shallows of the Anduin. It is a sign
of more prosperous times, when merchant-men trafficked their wares along the
great waterway and upriver passage relied on portage around the cataracts in the
Anduin. Westwards, across the turbulent stretch of river, the imposing image of
the Misty Mountains marching away from north to south dominates the horizon.
name: A Stand of Alders at the Anduin Bank

desc:
Hidden among the branches and thickets of the bank, a small cave has been
hollowed out over the years. The walls are packed dirt and the earthen floor is
covered with debris and remnants from all the inhabitants that have used the
cave for shelter throughout the years. A few large pawprints can be glimpsed in
the mess on the floor, indicating that the current occupants are somewhat larger
than a normal man.
name: A Hillside Cave

desc:
The tunnel comes to an abrupt stop here, forcing one to halt any advance. The
air still moves slowly, possibly finding small cracks to be drawn through. All
over the rocky floor there is the littering of bats and the remnants of various
smaller animals together with decaying pieces of wood.
name: A Dead End

desc:
The air is moist here, and a faint draft can be felt coming from the tunnels to
the west. The floor is covered with the littering of bats and the remnants of
some of their prey. Tunnels snake outwards in almost all directions, placing
this room as a small, dark nexus of these caves.
name: A Dry Escape

desc:
A dark and silent tunnel lies here, blocked by fallen rocks and a caved-in
ceiling. Among the rocks and boulders there are several thick strands of web
woven. A few strands of web have been drawn across the entire passage, making
travel here somewhat arduous and slow.
name: Webbed Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel leading here slowly opens up to a small cave that quickly narrows at
the northern end, blocking off any further progress. The air is still and thick
with the odour of the bat droppings that cover virtually everything in the dark
and gloomy cavern. The echoes of flapping wings and a faint noise of moving
water sound throughout this part of cave.
name: A Narrowing Cavern

desc:
Waves lap at the rocks in this part of this rocky, sloping tunnel, covering most
of the cave. The air is thick with moisture and the floor quickly vanishes
beneath the surface as the cavern plunges downward to the west. The rocks rise
eastward, allowing escape to drier and higher parts of this hollowed-out cavern
system beneath the Anduin.
name: Watery Cavern

desc:
The cave is completely filled with water and a small tug can be felt, as a weak
undercurrent is dragging and pulling, when following its course. Once sharp, the
rocks have slowly been withered away, creating rounded and smooth features in
this large underwater chamber.
name: Below the Anduin

desc:
A rickety ladder leads into a small room with earthen walls and stony floor. A
few shaky shelves lean against the wall, covered by a thick layer of dust. There
is an old rack barely holding together up against the northern wall that
contains a few farming tools that were once used many years ago when this farm
house was in use and prosperous. There is a trickle of water running down the
eastern wall forming a small puddle on the ground. The air is damp and musty.
name: A Musty Cellar

desc:
A wide, serpentine tunnel makes its way down into the heart of the hill, and
opens suddenly into a cavern, whose size is difficult to guess in the shadows.
The cave ceiling appears to be irregular, but it seems somewhat higher near the
middle, like some sort of a natural vault. The walls are made out of
coarse-grained, dark, damp earth, and they show sign of many several-inch-deep
scratches. On the other hand, small pools and black stains dot the ground here
and there, obviously due to the filtering water. But the atmosphere remains
always fresh, sheltered by many feet of rock and earth.
name: An Earthy Cave under the Hill

desc:
The cave appears to have been carved out of the same rock that runs underneath
the waters of the mighty Anduin, be it by the hand of man or by the force of
nature. The surface of the walls is rough, and blackened with ash, most probably
from the torchlight and campfires of previous travellers. In here, one could
rest on the pebbled surface of the ground, shielded from the outside, in
relative comfort.
name: A Wholesome Cave inside the Carrock

desc:
The dark and dry cave seems to resemble a home that would belong to a family of
bears, judging by the massive paw marks indented into the ground. Also on the
ground are many broken bones along with shreds of cloth that appear to have been
garments of clothing. There is a strong stench of decaying carcasses all around
you. The ground is very soft and moist and every step you take seems to create
an imprint of your footing.
name: A Bear's Cave in the Ridge

desc:
Although this small hillock rises only a few yards above the plains, and its
hillsides are long and gentle, it still stands lonely out of the great, green,
and drearily flat meadow. Its southern face appears to be slightly steeper, and,
surprisingly, the stones are numerous and the soil is rocky and hard, but the
grass still grows everywhere, and sometimes it even seems to sprout out of the
very rocks.
name: A Lonely Hill

desc:
At some point in history, this structure housed the protectors of this village.
That must have happened long ago though, as is clear from the present state of
things. Nothing here has been left intact. The broken walls barely provide
enough support for the collapsing roof, and the beds are in no better condition
than the walls they stand next to. The floor is cluttered with pieces of torn
mattresses and the fragments of what used to be a small table. Against the
western wall there is a weapons rack, now smashed apart and unable to hold any
weapons. Perhaps looters caused this destruction after the village was
abandoned, but from the look of it the village was abandoned because of this
great destruction.
name: Ruined Barracks

desc:
In this slight hollow of the ground, the grass seems to have gathered even more
densely than in the plains nearby. The stems sprout high and strong, up to
several feet, and their edges, straight and sharp, cut like knives. Be it due to
the abundant rain, the damp, or the water in the soil, the grass is always wet,
almost soaked. But nothing attracts your attention as much as its green colour,
deep and pure, and especially under the sunlight, when it shines so intensely
that it overwhelms your eyes, remaining so even after you close them.
name: Amidst the Grass

desc:
The land slowly descends to the great river flowing directly east of here. The
small stream is coming from the west and succumbs into the Anduin, where there
is a huge, lonely rock standing right in the middle of its course. Past the
rocky hill, the river seems to gently bend westwards. To the north, grasslands
seem to stretch out in the distance for a few miles.
name: A Stream through the Grasslands

desc:
An old oak-wood spreads all over this area. The tree branches are long and
twisted under the weight of their leafy tops, and the trunks look strong and
thick, with their wrinkled barks hardened through the years. Further east,
beyond them, stands a high, prickly, dark green hedge, so dense that you can not
discern anything on the other side, and the oaks follow its perimeter forming a
large belt. A broad and heavy wooden gate, beyond which you can see
elegant-looking gardens, opens through the hedge, and a grassy path starts there
and leads westwards, in the shade of some trees planted alongside in a row.
name: A Belt of Tall and Very Ancient Oaks

desc:
Flowing more calmly than the waters to the north, the mighty flow of the Anduin
follows the curve of the land, gracefully arching in a wide bend from north to
east. The steel-grey surface of the river reflects the towering, snow-clad peaks
of the Misty Mountains, their forested slopes and foothills marching swiftly
away to the west. Eastwards, beyond the next bend in the river, the grassy
plains of the Carrock Vale stretch out to the distant horizon, dotted with small
stands of trees and the occasional trickling meadow stream.
name: A Great Bend in the River Anduin

desc:
The powerful flow of the Anduin, originating from the distant glacier-capped
peaks of the Ered Mithrin and fed by numerous streams and rivers from the
eastern flanks of the Misty Mountains, completes a wide sweep from west to south
here. A cool and refreshing breeze glides across the rippled surface of the
steel-grey water as the river's soulful song plays in your ear. In wave after
green wave, the grasslands to the east roll out under the open vault of the sky.
name: A Mighty Sweep in the River Anduin
